rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOE W. THORN SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/17/21 for an appearance. No more hearing spots available on 1/25/21. The figures shown in paragraph 3 of the Third Supplement filed on 1/12/21 are not exactly supported by (or agree with) the bank statements and the portion of a spreadsheet for SW3 Associated attached as Exhibit C. Additionally, the executor still has not addressed what the status of opening an Oregon proceeding is and what her plans are re purchase or distribution of the Ashland property. (It is also noted that the numerous cash bequests do not appear to have been made yet, more than two years after the decedent's death; no petition for preliminary distribution has been filed. Interest has been accruing on those bequests. [Probate Code Section 12001 and 12003.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES JOSEPH DALY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/1/21. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just currently. Please file a revised declaration re the original account statements. LRSF 2.11 M.4 provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." A small estate affidavit must be filed conventionally with a certified copy of the death certificate attached. [LRSF 14.60 E.] The small estate affidavit is also required for the trustees of Florence Eleanor Weinstein's trust. Letters certified within 60 days and issued to the personal representative of Sheila Aileen Thorson's estate must also be filed conventionally. [LRSF 14.36 G.] (The interests of the stepchildren were not "intestate" interests, as they take under the decedent's will.) The fee calculation is incorrect. It is also unclear why the amounts set forth in paragraph 24 of the petition do not to correspond at all with the incorrect fee calculation shown in paragraph 23. Did the personal representative and her attorney agree to a lesser amount? The accounting schedules are not fully detailed: the 9 separate Series EE bonds were lumped together. The accounting also shows a gain on sale of the Fidelity money market account, but that account was shown on Attachment 1 of the I&A, which should include only cash or cash equivalents. If the Fidelity account was not a cash account, it should have been appraised by the Probate Referee and included on Attachment 2. (Item 4 of an I&A should not be answered when a Probate Referee was appointed. Paragraphs 11 and 12 of the petition appear to contradict each other. This is actually the first and final account and report.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/19/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition To Determine Title To And Require Trustee To Transfer Personal Property To Estate (Filed By La Tonya Carpenter In Consolidated Case Ptr-20-303434 On January 16, 2020)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/2021. Issues: 1) Amended petition filed 01/20/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 4pm on 02/15/2021. (LRSF 2.7, 14.7). 2) Petitioner only gave 5 days notice of the amended petition. 30 days notice is required.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MACPRIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (403 W. Orange Ave, South San Francisco, Ca 94080)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. Issues: 1. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Hearing for the proof of service. (Proof of posting is different) 2. Petitioner has not filed proof of publication. 3. Petitioner alleges appraised value is $1,100,000.00 but no I&A has been filed and no referee assigned. 4. Petitioner's bond was set at $10,000.00. Petitioner on page 2 under #6 alleges no additional bond is needed. Appears additional bond of $1,210,000.00 is needed. 5. The address under #2d appears to be incorrect. 6. Petitioner has not provided the required hard copy of the proposed order on judicial council form DE-265.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN M. WEBB REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendment And Remove Trustee
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the parties to update the Court on progress in this matter. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CANTILLANO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/1/21. Berta Quezada Quezada, as a beneficiary named in the trust, needed only to be given notice of the hearing along with a copy of the petition. There are other heirs, who are not named in the trust, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by the petition, who must be personally served notice or must waive notice. [Probate Code Section 17203.] Notice is still very much an issue.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL HOY WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/3/2021. Issues: 1) Petitioner has not filed proof of publication. Matter continued to allow time to publish. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.]
